info.title: Intent.SoftwareFactory.CLI version:
info.description: A CLI (command line interface) version of the Intent Architect Software Factory.

commands.name: apply-pending-changes
commands.description: Runs the Software Factory and applies any outstanding changes.

options.name: --application-id
options.description: The Id of the Intent Architect application. If unspecified then all applications found in the .isln will be run.

options.name: --attach-debugger
options.description: The Software Factory will pause at startup giving you chance to attach a .NET debugger.

options.name: --error-logging-command
options.description:
Command to use for logging an error. Some continuous integration environments watch output for \"commands\" for logging of errors. Will be automatically configured when the process is detected to be running on the following kinds of build servers:
- Azure Pipelines: By default applies \"##vso[task.logissue type=error;]{@m} {@x}\n\" (see https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devops/pipelines/scripts/logging-commands#logissue-log-an-error-or-warning)
See the documentation on Serilog.Expressions ExpressionTemplate for formatting options: https://github.com/serilog/serilog-expressions#formatting-with-expressiontemplate"

options.name: --warning-logging-command
options.description:
Command to use for logging a warning. Some continuous integration environments watch output for \"commands\" for logging of warnings. Will be automatically configured when the process is detected to be running on the following kinds of build servers:
- Azure Pipelines: By default applies \"##vso[task.logissue type=warning;]{@m} {@x}\n\" (see https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devops/pipelines/scripts/logging-commands#logissue-log-an-error-or-warning)
See the documentation on Serilog.Expressions ExpressionTemplate for formatting options: https://github.com/serilog/serilog-expressions#formatting-with-expressiontemplate"

arguments.name: USERNAME
arguments.description: Username for an active Intent Architect account. If you're using an Organization Access Token (OAT), use "token", see https://intentarchitect.com/redirect/GhQc8rW6 for more information.

arguments.name: PASSWORD
arguments.description: Password for the Intent Architect account. If a password is causing a "response file not found" error see https://intentarchitect.com/redirect/xwTSFCW9 for more information and a workaround.

arguments.name: ISLN_PATH
arguments.description: Path to the Intent Architect solution (.isln) file or folder containing a single .isln file.

commands.name: ensure-no-outstanding-changes
commands.description: Runs the Software Factory and if there are any outstanding changes it prints out an error and exits with a non-zero return code.

options.name: --application-id
options.description: The Id of the Intent Architect application. If unspecified then all applications found in the .isln will be run.

options.name: --attach-debugger
options.description: The Software Factory will pause at startup giving you chance to attach a .NET debugger.

options.name: --check-for-unapproved-customizations
options.description: Whether to also check for any unapproved customizations.

options.name: --continue-on-error
options.description: Whether Software Factory execution should continue to run for other applications when an error is encountered.

options.name: --error-logging-command
options.description:
Command to use for logging an error. Some continuous integration environments watch output for \"commands\" for logging of errors. Will be automatically configured when the process is detected to be running on the following kinds of build servers:
- Azure Pipelines: By default applies \"##vso[task.logissue type=error;]{@m} {@x}\n\" (see https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devops/pipelines/scripts/logging-commands#logissue-log-an-error-or-warning)
See the documentation on Serilog.Expressions ExpressionTemplate for formatting options: https://github.com/serilog/serilog-expressions#formatting-with-expressiontemplate"

options.name: --warning-logging-command
options.description:
Command to use for logging a warning. Some continuous integration environments watch output for \"commands\" for logging of warnings. Will be automatically configured when the process is detected to be running on the following kinds of build servers:
- Azure Pipelines: By default applies \"##vso[task.logissue type=warning;]{@m} {@x}\n\" (see https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devops/pipelines/scripts/logging-commands#logissue-log-an-error-or-warning)
See the documentation on Serilog.Expressions ExpressionTemplate for formatting options: https://github.com/serilog/serilog-expressions#formatting-with-expressiontemplate"
